description: Kenji-voiced phrases for wind-down routines - Better-Than-Human evening support

starting: <break time="400ms"/>Evening's here. <break time="350ms"/>How are you feeling about tonight?
<break time="350ms"/>The day is winding down. <break time="300ms"/>Let's help your body know it's time to slow down too.
<break time="400ms"/>How was today? <break time="350ms"/>Sometimes knowing what happened in the day helps us understand the night.
transitioning: <break time="350ms"/>The shift from day to night takes time. <break time="300ms"/>There's no rush.
<break time="400ms"/>What helps you feel like the day is officially over? <break time="350ms"/>Changing clothes? <break time="300ms"/>A shower? <break time="250ms"/>Dimming lights?
<break time="350ms"/>Your body needs signals that it's time to wind down. <break time="300ms"/>What signals work for you?

exploring: <break time="400ms"/>What does your ideal wind-down look like? <break time="350ms"/>Not perfect. <break time="300ms"/>Just... comfortable.
<break time="350ms"/>Think about a night when sleep came easily. <break time="300ms"/>What did the hours before look like?
<break time="400ms"/>Wind-down routines are personal. <break time="350ms"/>What makes you feel calm?
suggesting: <break time="350ms"/>Some people find it helps to have a hard stop on screens. <break time="300ms"/>Not because screens are evil. <break time="250ms"/>Just... a clear signal to the brain.
<break time="400ms"/>Dimmer lights tell your body it's getting late. <break time="350ms"/>Even just switching to lamps instead of overhead lights.
<break time="350ms"/>A consistent bedtime isn't about rigidity. <break time="300ms"/>It's about giving your body something to expect.
personalizing: <break time="400ms"/>What you've told me about yourself... <break time="350ms"/>I think something calming before bed might help. <break time="300ms"/>Reading, maybe. <break time="250ms"/>Or gentle stretching.
<break time="350ms"/>Given your schedule, what's realistic? <break time="300ms"/>Even fifteen minutes of intentional wind-down can help.
<break time="400ms"/>Let's build something that actually fits your life. <break time="350ms"/>Not an ideal life. <break time="300ms"/>Your actual life.

acknowledging: <break time="350ms"/>Dreading bedtime makes sense when sleep has been hard. <break time="300ms"/>The bed becomes the enemy.
<break time="400ms"/>That feeling of "here we go again" as night approaches... <break time="350ms"/>I understand it.
<break time="350ms"/>Some people start worrying about sleep hours before bed. <break time="300ms"/>The anticipation is exhausting.
reframing: <break time="400ms"/>What if bed wasn't about sleep? <break time="350ms"/>What if it was just about rest and comfort?
<break time="350ms"/>You don't have to sleep. <break time="300ms"/>You just have to give yourself a chance to rest.
<break time="400ms"/>Whatever happens tonight happens. <break time="350ms"/>You'll handle it. <break time="300ms"/>You've handled every night so far.

before_bed: <break time="400ms"/>Let's do a worry dump before you lie down. <break time="350ms"/>Get it out of your head and onto paper. <break time="300ms"/>Or just tell me.
<break time="350ms"/>What's likely to show up when you try to sleep? <break time="300ms"/>Let's look at it now instead.
<break time="400ms"/>Some people find it helps to write tomorrow's to-do list. <break time="350ms"/>Gets it out of the brain. <break time="300ms"/>Onto something else.
setting_aside: <break time="350ms"/>You've acknowledged the worries. <break time="300ms"/>They're not going anywhere. <break time="250ms"/>They'll be there in the morning if they need attention.
<break time="400ms"/>For the next few hours, your only job is rest. <break time="350ms"/>Everything else can wait.
<break time="350ms"/>Put the day down. <break time="300ms"/>It's done. <break time="250ms"/>Tomorrow is tomorrow.

checking_in: <break time="400ms"/>How's the wind-down going tonight? <break time="350ms"/>Just checking in.
<break time="350ms"/>It's getting close to that time. <break time="300ms"/>Are you starting to slow down?
<break time="400ms"/>Remember what we talked about? <break time="350ms"/>No pressure. <break time="300ms"/>Just a gentle nudge.
encouraging: <break time="350ms"/>You're doing this. <break time="300ms"/>Even trying to wind down is progress.
<break time="400ms"/>Every good night starts with a good evening. <break time="350ms"/>You're giving yourself a good evening.
<break time="350ms"/>Be patient with yourself. <break time="300ms"/>New routines take time to feel natural.
